summary: PRED-090 claims NH TOA flux excess >0.3 W/m². CERES EBAF data (published before registration) already shows NH excess of ~0.54 W/m² for 2020-2024. This is textbook retrodiction.

prediction.claim: Northern hemisphere TOA flux excess >0.3 W/m² in April 2026

evidence.ceres_data: CERES EBAF Ed4.2 covers March 2000 through February 2025. The 25-year record was publicly available before PRED-090 registration.
evidence.pnas_paper: Loeb et al., "Emerging hemispheric asymmetry of Earth's radiation" (PNAS, 2025, doi:10.1073/pnas.2511595122) reports NH-SH trend difference of 0.34 ± 0.23 W/m² per decade in absorbed solar radiation.
evidence.specific_numbers: During 2020-2024, the NH average absorbed solar radiation exceeded SH by 0.54 W/m² — well above the 0.3 W/m² threshold claimed by PRED-090.
evidence.prior_data: The hemispheric asymmetry has been documented in peer-reviewed literature since at least Stephens et al. (2015) and Loeb et al. (2020). The >0.3 W/m² NH excess is not a novel prediction.
evidence.data_availability: CERES monthly composites through early 2025 were publicly available from NASA ASDC before PRED-090 was registered on 2026-04-04.

assessment.kernel_of_truth: The NH-SH asymmetry IS real and IS growing. CERES data confirms the NH absorbs more solar radiation. The dome model is citing a genuine geophysical trend. But this is standard radiative budget science — the asymmetry is driven by NH sea-ice loss, snow cover reduction, and aerosol changes, all well-explained by standard climate physics. The dome attributes it to aetheric mechanisms, but the prediction merely restates published CERES findings.
assessment.strongest_argument: PRED-090 was registered 2026-04-04. The PNAS paper (2025) documenting NH excess of 0.54 W/m² was published months before registration. CERES EBAF Ed4.2 data through Feb 2025 was publicly downloadable. Setting a threshold of >0.3 W/m² when the measured value is already 0.54 W/m² is not a prediction — it is a loose re-statement of a known measurement with a safe margin.
assessment.relation_to_win066: Restates WIN-066 (heat excess trend), which our review already covers. PRED-090 adds nothing new — it timestamps a claim that was already in the confirmed WINs list.

recommendation: Update PRED-090 in predictions.json: set outcome to "confirmed" (the number IS >0.3), but update our_verdict to indicate post-hoc retrodiction. Note for Part 6 prose: this is a prime example of crypto-timestamping a known measurement and calling it a prospective prediction. The author's own "What Prospective Means" framework would classify this as BACKTESTED.
action_for_decider: Close ISS-701. Update PRED-090 outcome. Include in Wave 3 writeup as example of timestamped retrodiction.
